action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 3 segfaulted again (exit 139) during train.py, despite using a pure-PyTorch model and num_workers=0. The inline forward test passed, so the crash occurs inside the training loop. Primary suspect: torch.bincount on a CUDA tensor (only works on CPU in many PyTorch versions and can segfault). Additional risk: torch_geometric DataLoader may still invoke compiled C++ code during collate. Analysis: I will eliminate both risks by (1) vectorizing the model across the batch with padding/masking, removing the need for torch.bincount and per-sample Python loops, and (2) replacing PyG DataLoader with standard torch.utils.data.DataLoader and a custom collate function. This should be both safer and much faster. Decision: Rewrite model.py, train.py, and predict.py with fully-vectorized batching and pure PyTorch ops. Run a 1-epoch test on 2,000 samples to confirm no segfault and measure speed before scaling up.
